name: Mexican Jalapeno Chocolate Cream Cake
recipeIngredient: 3 large Egg yolks, at room temperature
2 medium Jalapeno peppers, seeded and de-ribbed, finely minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Butter a round cake pan. Line the bottom with wax paper and butter it. Set aside.
3. In a small, heavy saucepan over the lowest possible heat (or in the top of a double boiler set over simmering water), melt chocolate with Kahlua. Stir until smooth and set aside to cool.
4. In a large bowl, cream together butter and 3/4 cup sugar until thick and pale yellow. Add egg yolks one at a time, beating well after each addition until completely incorporated. Turn the speed to low and mix in the chocolate mixture until thoroughly combined. Blend in vanilla extract.
5. Add flour, cinnamon, almonds, and jalapeno peppers; continue beating until well combined.
6. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites with a pinch of salt until soft peaks form. Gradually add remaining tablespoon sugar, continuing to beat until soft peaks are formed.
7. With a rubber spatula, fold one-third of the egg whites into the chocolate batter until thoroughly combined. Fold in the remaining egg whites gently and continue folding until no streaks of white remain.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 25 minutes in the center of the oven. The cake will appear soft and underbaked in the center, but this is desired.
9. Let cool in the pan on a wire rack to room temperature or at least half an hour.
10. Remove from pan and invert onto a plate or cake stand. Serve plain, dusted with confectioners' sugar, and topped with Kahlua-flavored whipped cream.
Serves 6-8
